summary: Stage decomp/matching PR touching Green Greens, Pura, and Push On ground code. It adds or improves several stage functions, updates ground variable types and headers, and adjusts a Green Greens data split. Bot report records +1960 matched code bytes, +32 matched data bytes, 8 new matches, 17 unmatched-item improvements, and 2 Green Greens unmatched-item regressions.

key_files.role: Largest source change; decompiled/cleaned multiple Pura functions, added typed accesses, camera subject handling, HSD_ASSERT use, and matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and noinline wrappers.
Added implementation for grPushOn_80218888 and matched fn_802192A4; added vector/math includes and an extern Vec3 used in light-position logic.
Green Greens matching cleanup and behavior/data adjustments: block accessor cast, new index table, bomb helper, row/column access changes, swaps via getBlock, and weight switch reorder.
Header signature cleanup and typed data declaration for grPu_803E6C0C as GrPuVtxMapEntry[42].
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_802192A4 with s32 fn_802192A4(void*, HSD_GObj*, s32*).
Updated Pura ground variable field types and added grPura_GroundVars3 with HSD_JObj and CmSubject arrays to Ground union.
Split grGr_803E787C symbol size from 0xC4 to 0x3C and added grGr_803E78B8 at .data:0x803E78B8 size 0x88.

what_changed: Added or improved source for Pura functions including grPura_80211D00, grPura_802120E0, grPura_8021231C, grPura_802125F0, grPura_80212CD4, grPura_80212EF4, grPura_80212FC0, grPura_80213030, and grPura_80213250.

Added full source for Push On lighting/selection routine grPushOn_80218888 and source for fn_802192A4, with the bot report listing fn_802192A4 as a new 100% match and grPushOn_80218888 as improved to 92.87%.
Replaced Pura raw array/M2C_FIELD style accesses to grPu_803E6C0C with a GrPuVtxMapEntry struct and typed indexed fields.
Updated headers to expose recovered signatures for Pura and Push On functions instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where changed.
Changed Pura GroundVars typing: xC4 to s16, xC8 to s16, added grPura_GroundVars3 arrays for JObjs and camera subjects.

Green Greens changes include a 2D-array cast in getBlock, a new grGr_803E787C index table, switch-case row weighting reorder, block coordinate order changes in grGreens_802159B8, and a helper-like BOMB inline for repeated bomb initialization calls.
Adjusted GALE01 symbol metadata by splitting grGr_803E787C and adding grGr_803E78B8.
Bot report says new matches include fn_802192A4, grPura_80212EF4, grPura_8021231C, fn_80216DE4, grPura .sdata, grPura_80212FC0, grPura_80213030, and grPura_80211D00.
smart_moves: Typed the Pura vertex map data as GrPuVtxMapEntry and then used field accesses in grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 instead of raw u16 pointer stepping and M2C_FIELD.
Updated owning headers when function signatures were recovered, particularly fn_802192A4 and grPura_80212CD4/grPura_80212EF4/grPura_80213250.
Used HSD_ASSERT in grPura_802125F0 after including baselib/debug.h, replacing raw __assert blocks in the diff excerpt.
Used local helper/accessor patterns such as getBlock and GET_JOBJ to make stage code less offset-driven where the diff supports the role.
Kept stage-specific data/symbol changes narrow: the visible config edit only adjusts one Green Greens data symbol size and adds one adjacent symbol.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an array-like data symbol is repeatedly accessed by fixed record stride, recovering a small struct type can remove M2C_FIELD and pointer-step code while improving reviewability.
Function-body recovery should be paired with header prototype cleanup when the signature becomes concrete.
For grid-like stage state, a typed helper/accessor can make row/column operations and struct swaps clearer than manual byte or stride arithmetic.

naming_conventions.name: GrPuVtxMapEntry
naming_conventions.convention: Stage-prefixed struct typedef for a recovered Pura vertex/JObj mapping record.

naming_conventions.name: grPura_80211E08_noinline / grPura_80211E08_noinline2
naming_conventions.convention: Explicit noinline-style wrapper names document a matching/codegen tactic rather than inferred game semantics.

naming_conventions.name: left_max, left_min, left_top, left_bottom, right_max, right_min, right_top, right_bottom
naming_conventions.convention: Directional extrema locals are used in Green Greens boundary calculations.

naming_conventions.name: child, iter, jobj
naming_conventions.convention: Pura JObj traversal cleanup replaces more generic arg/temp-style naming where roles are clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in grPura_802120E0 and grPushOn_80218888, indicating stack-shape steering for matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not include objdiff details for why each PAD_STACK was necessary; keep as PR-specific tactic evidence, not a global style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Noinline wrapper chain grPura_80211E08_noinline -> grPura_80211E08_noinline2 was introduced and used in grPura_802125F0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The name and call placement support a matching tactic, but no reviewer explanation or verifier trace is present.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Repeated BOMB calls replaced an index array loop in grGreens_80214B58, improving the bot score for that function to 99.26% but creating generated-looking unrolled calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This conflicts with the natural-loops standard unless backed by verifier evidence; PR evidence only shows score improvement, not rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline numeric float maxima such as 3.4028235e38f replaced F32_MAX in grGreens_8021483C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded PR evidence does not explain why macro removal was needed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A local data-symbol split in symbols.txt plus a new static s16 table grGr_803E787C supported Green Greens .data improvement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as a candidate path/data fact, not a general license to split data symbols without ownership evidence.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report: matched code 69.18% (+0.05%, +1960 bytes), matched data 41.19% (+0.00%, +32 bytes), 8 new matches, 17 improvements in unmatched items, and 2 regressions in unmatched Green Greens items.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Reported regressions: main/melee/gr/grgreens grGreens_80215ED8 -12 bytes, fn_80215B84 -6 bytes.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching grPura_80211E08_noinline or grPura_80211E08_noinline2 to see whether the noinline wrappers were cleaned up or kept.
Search for later cleanup of grGreens_80214B58 BOMB unrolled calls or restoration of a natural loop/index table.
Search for grPu_803E6C0C and GrPuVtxMapEntry in later changes to confirm whether the typed data model remained stable.
Check later Green Greens PRs for fixes to bot-reported regressions in grGreens_80215ED8 and fn_80215B84.
Search for grPushOn_803B844C extern placement to determine whether it was later moved to an owning header or data declaration.

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The bot report provides matching deltas and new-match/improvement/regression lists, but no narrow objdiff logs or author rationale.
The raw diff in loaded_files was truncated; targeted local reads recovered the header, Push On, and types hunks needed for this intake.
Several tactics are visible in source, including PAD_STACK, noinline wrappers, literal float changes, and unrolled BOMB calls, but the loaded PR evidence does not prove why each was required.
Merge state is supported by metadata, but acceptance of any tactic as a global standard is not inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed record recovery removed M2C_FIELD in Pura vertex map code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2534 recovered grPu_803E6C0C as GrPuVtxMapEntry[42] and updated grPura_80212FC0/grPura_80213030 to use typed fields instead of raw u16 pointer stepping and M2C_FIELD.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered function signatures should be reflected in owning headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2534 changed fn_802192A4 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 fn_802192A4(void*, HSD_GObj*, s32*) in grpushon.h and updated several Pura prototypes after adding bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: Green Greens data symbol split in PR 2534
source_update_candidates.text: config/GALE01/symbols.txt changed grGr_803E787C size from 0xC4 to 0x3C and added grGr_803E78B8 at .data:0x803E78B8 size 0x88.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is symbol metadata/data ownership information and should be curated as a path fact only after checking current symbols/splits.

source_update_candidates.title: Tactics with visible score gains still need verifier rationale
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2534 visibly used PAD_STACK, noinline wrappers, literal float substitutions, and unrolled calls. The bot report shows matching improvements, but the slice lacks narrow objdiff rationale for each tactic.
source_update_candidates.reason: This supports reinforcing the existing standard that matching tactics require evidence; it should not be promoted as a new accepted tactic without more verification evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Pura ground vars typed in PR 2534
source_update_candidates.text: types.h changed grPura_GroundVars xC4 to s16, xC8 to s16, and added grPura_GroundVars3 containing HSD_JObj* xC4[25] and CmSubject* x128[25], then added pura3 to the Ground union.
source_update_candidates.reason: Type layout facts belong in source/path-fact curation and should be checked against current headers before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the BOMB unrolled-call shape as an accepted decomp style; PR evidence shows score improvement but not why a natural loop failed.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or intent; no human review comments are present.
Do not treat noinline wrappers or PAD_STACK as generally acceptable from this PR alone; they are visible tactics with insufficient rationale in the slice.
Do not claim all changed functions matched; the bot report distinguishes 8 new matches from 17 partial improvements and 2 regressions.
